steps.title: Find the clue in the passage
steps.content: Seacole writes that nature “determined to throw every conceivable obstacle” in the way of those crossing the Isthmus of Panama. The word “every” signals the full range of obstacles one could think of or imagine, so “conceivable” means able to be mentally formed or imagined.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Visible: Visible means able to be seen with the eyes — the passage says obstacles could be conceived of, a mental act, not that they were physically visible. ✗
  • Choice B — Steady: Steady means constant or unwavering — this describes persistence over time, not the range of obstacles one can imagine. ✗
  • Choice C — Easy: Easy means not difficult — this contradicts the passage, which frames the crossing as anything but easy. ✗
  • Choice D — Possible: Possible means able to exist or be imagined — “every possible obstacle” captures the same full range as “every conceivable obstacle.” ✓
That matches Choice D — Possible.
